title: Pre-Defined Report and Dashboard Views with Saved Filter Presets
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want pre-defined views that apply common filter combinations, so that I can quickly switch between useful perspectives on my reports and dashboards without manually setting filters each time.
description:
Introduces a Views dropdown that provides pre-configured filter presets for common use cases:

- **My Reports** — Created by current user, Analytics type = Reports
- **My Dashboards** — Created by current user, Analytics type = Dashboards
- **My Work in Progress** — Created by current user, Status = In-Progress
- **My Recently Modified** — Modified by current user, Last modified within last 7 days
- **Production Monthly Active Analytics** — Status = Production, Last Viewed within last 30 days
- **Recommended for Deprecation** — Last Viewed > 90 days, excluding Status = Deprecated (invisible filter)

Each view applies the specified filter combination automatically. The "Recommended for Deprecation" view includes an invisible filter that excludes already-deprecated items, and requires a "> 90 days" option in the custom date filter controls.
acceptance_criteria: Views dropdown is available on the Reports and Dashboards page
Each pre-defined view applies the correct filter combination
My Reports and My Dashboards filter by current user's created by
My Work in Progress filters by current user and In-Progress status
My Recently Modified filters by current user and last 7 days modified date
Production Monthly Active filters by Production status and 30-day last viewed
Recommended for Deprecation shows items not viewed in 90+ days, excluding deprecated
Views can be combined with manual filter adjustments
ux_changes: Added a Views dropdown with pre-defined filter presets. Each view auto-applies a specific filter combination. Added '>90 days' option to custom date filter controls.
middleware_changes: New views model specific to reports/dashboards that maps view names to filter combinations. Logic to apply invisible filters (exclude deprecated) for specific views.
implementation_notes: Waqar handled this task because it involved a new views model specific to reports/dashboards. The duplicate 'My Reports' and 'My Dashboards' entries in the original spec were a typo — each is listed once.
comment_summary: Ryan assigned to Waqar due to the new views model requirement. Waqar implemented and noted the duplicate filter names in the spec. Requested feedback on development.
